author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

ccel.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm LXXVI

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Jews are wont to glory in this Psalm which we have sung, saying, “Known in Judæa is God, in Israel great is the name of Him:” and to revile the Gentiles to whom God is not known, and to say that to themselves alone God is known; seeing that the Prophet saith, “Known in Judæa is God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In other places therefore He is unknown.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But God is known in very deed in Judæa, if they understand what is Judæa.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For indeed God is not known except in Judæa.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold even we say this, that except a person shall have been in Judæa, known to him God cannot be.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But what saith the Apostle?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He that in secret is a Jew, he that is so in circumcision of the heart, not in letter but in spirit.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There are therefore Jews in circumcision of the flesh, and there are Jews in circumcision of the heart.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Many of our holy fathers had both the circumcision of the flesh, for a seal of the faith, and circumcision of the heart, for the faith itself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: From these fathers these men degenerating, who now in the name do glory, and have lost their deeds; from these fathers, I say, degenerating, they have remained Jews in flesh, in heart Heathens.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For these are Jews, who are out of Abraham, from whom Isaac was born, and out of him Jacob, and out of Jacob the twelve Patriarchs, and out of the twelve Patriarchs the whole people of the Jews.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But they were generally called Jews for this reason, that Judah was one of the twelve sons of Jacob, a Patriarch among the twelve, and from his stock the Royalty came among the Jews.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For all this people after the number of the twelve sons of Jacob, had twelve tribes.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What we call tribes are as it were distinct houses and congregations of people.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That people, I say, had twelve tribes, out of which twelve tribes one tribe was Judah, out of which were the kings; and there was another tribe, Levi, out of which were the priests.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But because to the priests serving the temple no land was allotted, but it was necessary that among twelve tribes all the Land of promise should be shared: there having been therefore taken out one tribe of higher dignity, the tribe of Levi, which was of the priests, there would have remained eleven, unless by the adoption of the two sons of Joseph the number twelve were completed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What this is, observe.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: One of the twelve sons of Jacob was Joseph.…This Joseph had two sons, Ephraim and Manasse.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Jacob, dying, as though by will, received those his grandsons into the number of sons, and said to his son Joseph, “The rest that are born shall be to thee; but these to me, and they shall divide the land with their brethren.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: As yet there had not been given nor divided the land of promise, but he was speaking in the Spirit, prophesying.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The two sons therefore of Joseph being added, there were made up nevertheless twelve tribes, since now there are thirteen.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For instead of one tribe of Joseph, two were added, and there were made thirteen.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There being taken out then the tribe of Levi, that tribe of priests which did serve the Temple, and lived by the tithes of all the rest unto whom the land was divided, there remain twelve.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In these twelve was the tribe of Judah, whence the kings were.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For at first from another tribe was given King Saul, and he was rejected as being an evil king; after there was given from the tribe of Judah King David, and out of him from the tribe of Judah were the Kings.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But Jacob had spoken of this, when he blessed his sons, “there shall not fail a prince out of Judah, nor a leader from his thighs, until there come He to whom the promise hath been made.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But from the tribe of Judah there came Our Lord Jesus Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For He is, as the Scripture saith, and as ye have but now heard, out of the seed of David born of Mary.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But as regardeth the Divinity of our Lord Jesus Christ, wherein He is equal with the Father, He is not only before the Jews, but also before Abraham himself; nor only before Abraham, but also before Adam; nor only before Adam, but also before Heaven and earth and before ages: for all things by Himself were made, and without Him there was made nothing.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because therefore in prophecy hath been said, “there shall not fail a prince out of Judah,” etc.: former times are examined, and we find that the Jews always had their kings of the tribe of Judah, and had no foreign king before that Herod who was king when the Lord was born.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thence began foreign kings, from Herod.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Before Herod all were of the tribe of Judah, but only until there should come He to whom the promise had been made.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore when the Lord Himself came, the kingdom of the Jews was overthrown, and removed from the Jews.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now they have no king; because they will not acknowledge the true King.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: See now whether they must be called Jews.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now ye do see that they must not be called Jews.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They have themselves with their own voice resigned that name, so that they are not worthy to be called Jews, except only in the flesh.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When did they sever themselves from the name?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They said, “We have no king but Cæsar.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: O ye who are called Jews and are not, if ye have no king but Cæsar, there hath failed a Prince of Judah: there hath come then He to whom the promise hath been made.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They then are more truly Jews, who have been made Christians out of Jews: the rest of the Jews, who in Christ have not believed, have deserved to lose even the very name.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The true Judæa, then, is the Church of Christ, believing in that King, who hath come out of the tribe of Judah through the Virgin Mary; believing in Him of whom the Apostle was just now speaking, in writing to Timothy, “Be thou mindful that Jesus Christ hath risen from the dead, of the seed of David, after my Gospel.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For of Judah is David, and out of David is the Lord Jesus Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We believing in Christ do belong to Judah: and we acknowledge Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We, that with eyes have not seen, in faith do keep Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let not therefore the Jews revile, who are no longer Jews.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They said themselves, “We have no king but Cæsar.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For better were it for them that their king should be Christ, of the seed of David, of the tribe of Judah.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Nevertheless because Christ Himself is of the seed of David after the flesh, but God above all things blessed for ever, He is Himself our King and our God; our King, inasmuch as born of the tribe of Judah, after the flesh, was Christ the Lord, the Saviour; but our God, who is before Judah, and before Heaven and earth, by whom were made all things, both spiritual and corporal.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For if all things by Himself were made; even Mary herself, out of whom He was born, by Himself was made.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Known in Judæa is God, in Israel great is the Name of Him” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Concerning Israel also we ought so to take it as we have concerning Judæa: as they were not the true Jews, so neither was that the true Israel.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what is Israel said to be?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: One seeing God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And how have they seen God, among whom He walked in the flesh; and while they supposed Him to be man, they slew Him?…“In Israel great is His Name.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wilt thou be Israel?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Observe that man concerning whom the Lord saith, “Behold an Israelite indeed, in whom guile is not.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If a true Israelite is he in whom guile is not, the guileful and lying are not true Israelites.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let them not say then, that with them is God, and great is His name in Israel.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let them prove themselves Israelites, and I grant that “in Israel great is His Name.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And there hath been made in peace a place for Him, and His habitation is in Sion” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Again, Sion is as it were the country of the Jews; the true Sion is the Church of Christians.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But the interpretation of the Hebrew names is thus handed down to us: Judæa is interpreted confession, Israel, one seeing God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: After Judæa is Israel.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wilt thou see God?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: First do thou confess, and then in thyself there is made a place for God; because “there hath been made in peace a place for Him.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So long as then thou confessest not thy sins, in a manner thou art quarrelling with God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For how art thou not disputing with Him, who art praising that which displeaseth Him?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He punisheth a thief, thou dost praise theft: He doth punish a drunken man, thou dost praise drunkenness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou art disputing with God, thou hast not made for Him a place in thy heart: because in peace is His place.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And how dost thou begin to have peace with God?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou beginnest with Him in confession.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There is a voice of a Psalm, saying, “Begin ye to the Lord in confession.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, “Begin ye to the Lord in confession”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Begin ye to be joined to the Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In what manner?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So that the same thing may displease you as displeaseth Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There displeaseth Him thy evil life; if it please thyself, thou art disunited from Him; if it displease thee, through confession to Him thou art united.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “There He hath broken the strength of bows, and the shield, and the sword, and the battle” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Where hath He broken?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In that eternal peace, in that perfect peace.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And now, my brethren, they that have rightly believed see that they ought not to rely on themselves: and all the might of their own menaces, and whatsoever is in them whetted for mischief, this they break in pieces; and whatsoever they deem of great virtue wherewith to protect themselves temporally, and the war which they were waging against God by defending their sins, all these things He hath broken there.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou enlightening marvellously from the eternal mountains” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What are the eternal mountains?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Those which He hath Himself made eternal; which are the great mountains, the preachers of truth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou dost enlighten, but from the eternal mountains: the great mountains are first to receive Thy light, and from Thy light which the mountains receive, the earth also is clothed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But those great mountains the Apostles have received, the Apostles have received as it were the first streaks of the rising light.…Wherefore also, in another place, a Psalm saith what?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I have lifted up mine eyes unto the mountains, whence there shall come help to me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What then, in the mountains is thy hope, and from thence to thee shall there come help?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hast thou stayed at the mountains?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Take heed what thou doest.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There is something above the mountains: above the mountains is He at whom the mountains tremble.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I have lifted up,” he saith, “mine eyes unto the mountains, whence there shall come help to me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But what followeth?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “My help,” he saith, “is from the Lord, who hath made Heaven and earth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Unto the mountains indeed I have lifted up eyes, because through the mountains to me the Scriptures were displayed: but I have my heart in Him that doth enlighten all mountains.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “There have been troubled all the unwise in heart” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 5).…How have they been troubled?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When the Gospel is preached.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And what is life eternal?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And who is He that hath risen from the dead?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Athenians wondered, when the Apostle Paul spake of the resurrection of the dead, and thought that he spake but fables.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But because he said that there was another life which neither eye hath seen, nor ear heard, nor hath it gone up into the heart of man, therefore the unwise in heart were troubled.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But what hath befallen them?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “They have slept their sleep, and all men of riches have found nothing in their hands.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They have loved things present, and have gone to sleep in the midst of things present: and so these very present things have become to them delightful: just as he that seeth in a dream himself to have found treasure, is so long rich as he waketh not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The dream hath made him rich, waking hath made him poor.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Sleep perchance hath held him slumbering on the earth, and lying on the hard ground, poor and perchance a beggar; in sleep he hath seen himself to lie on an ivory or golden bed, and on feathers heaped up; so long as he is sleeping, he is sleeping well, waking he hath found himself on the hard ground, whereon sleep had taken him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Such men also are these too: they have come into this life, and through temporal desires, they have as it were slumbered here; and them riches, and vain pomps that fly away, have taken, and they have passed away: they have not understood how much of good might be done therewith.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For if they had known of another life, there they would have laid up unto themselves the treasure which here was doomed to perish: like as Zacchæus, the chief of the Publicans, saw that good when he received the Lord Jesus in his house, and he saith, “The half of my goods I give to the poor, and if to any man I have done any wrong, fourfold I restore.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This man was not in the emptiness of men dreaming, but in the faith of men awake.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “By Thy chiding, O God of Jacob, there have slept all men that have mounted horses” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who are they that have mounted horses?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They that would not be humble.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To sit on horseback is no sin; but it is a sin to lift up the neck of power against God, and to deem one’s self to be in some distinction.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because thou art rich, thou hast mounted; God doth chide, and thou sleepest.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Great is the anger of Him chiding, great the anger.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let your Love observe the terrible thing.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Chiding hath noise, the noise is wont to make men wake.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So great is the force of God chiding, that he said, “By Thy chiding, O God of Jacob, there have slept all men that have mounted horses.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold what a sleep that Pharaoh slept who mounted horses.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For he was not awake in heart, because against chiding he had his heart hardened.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For hardness of heart is slumber.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I ask you, my brethren, how they sleep, who, while the Gospel is sounding, and the Amen, and the Hallelujah, throughout the whole world, yet will not condemn their old life, and wake up unto a new life.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There was the Scripture of God in Judæa only, now throughout the whole world it is sung.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In that one nation one God who made all things was spoken of, as to be adored and worshipped; now where is He unsaid?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Christ hath risen again, though derided on the Cross; that very Cross whereon He was derided, He hath now imprinted on the brows of kings: and men yet sleep.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou art terrible, and who shall withstand Thee at that time by Thine anger?” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now they sleep, and perceive not Thee angry; but for cause that they should sleep, He was angry.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now that which sleeping they perceived not, at the end they shall perceive.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For there shall appear the Judge of quick and dead.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And who shall withstand Thee at that time by Thine anger?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For now they speak that which they will, and they dispute against God and say, who are the Christians? or who is Christ? or what fools are they that believe that which they see not, and relinquish the pleasures which they see, and follow the faith of things which are not displayed to their eyes!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Ye sleep and snore, ye speak against God, as much as ye are able.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “How long shall sinners, O Lord, how long shall sinners glory, they answer and will speak iniquity?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But when doth no one answer and no one speak, except when he turneth himself against himself?…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “From Heaven Thou hast hurled judgment: the earth hath trembled, and hath rested” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: She which now doth trouble herself, she which now speaketh, hath to fear at the end and to rest.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Better had she now rested, that at the end she might have rejoiced.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “When God arose unto judgment, that He might save all the meek in heart” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who are the meek in heart?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They that on snorting horses have not mounted, but in their humility have confessed their own sins.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For the thought of a man shall confess to Thee, and the remnants of the thought shall celebrate solemnities to Thee” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The first is the thought, the latter are the remnants of the thought.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is the first thought?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That from whence we begin, that good thought whence thou wilt begin to confess.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Confession uniteth us to Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But now the confession itself, that is, the first thought, doth produce in us the remnants of the thought: and those very “remnants of thought shall celebrate solemnities to Thee.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is the thought which shall confess?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That which condemneth the former life, that whereunto that which it was is displeasing, in order that it may be that which it was not, is itself the first thought.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But because thus thou oughtest to withdraw from sins, with the first thought after having confessed to God, that it may not escape thy memory that thou hast been a sinner; in that thou hast been a sinner, thou dost celebrate solemnities to God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Furthermore it is to be understood as followeth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The first thought hath confession, and departure from the old life.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But if thou shalt have forgotten from what sins thou hast been delivered, thou dost not render thanks to the Deliverer, and dost not celebrate solemnities to thy God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold the first confessing thought of Saul the Apostle, now Paul, who at first was Saul, when he heard a voice from Heaven!…He put forth the first thought of obedience: when he heard, “I am Jesus of Nazareth, whom thou persecutest,” “O Lord,” he saith, “what dost Thou bid me to do?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is a thought confessing: now he is calling upon the Lord, whom he persecuted.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In what manner the remnants of the thought shall celebrate solemnities, in the case of Paul ye have heard, when the Apostle himself was being read: “Be thou mindful that Christ Jesus hath risen from the dead, of the seed of David, after my Gospel.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, be thou mindful?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Though effaced from thy memory be the thought, whereby at first thou hast confessed: be the remnant of the thought in the memory.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Even once was Christ sacrificed for us, when we believed; then was thought; but now there are the remnants of thought, when we remember Who hath come to us, and what He hath forgiven us; by means of those very remnants of thought, that is, by means of the memory herself, He is daily so sacrificed for us, as if He were daily renewing us, that hath renewed us by His first grace.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For now the Lord hath renewed us in Baptism, and we have become new men, in hope indeed rejoicing, in order that in tribulation we may be patient: nevertheless, there ought not to escape from our memory that which hath been bestowed upon us.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And if now thy thought is not what it was,—for the first thought was to depart from sin: but now thou dost not depart, but at that time didst depart,—be there remnants of thought, lest He who hath made whole escape from memory.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Vow ye, and pay to the Lord our God” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let each man vow what he is able, and pay it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Do not vow and not pay: but let every man vow, and pay what he can.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Be ye not slow to vow: for ye will accomplish the vows by powers not your own.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Ye will fail, if on yourselves ye rely: but if on Him to whom ye vow ye rely, ye will be safe to pay.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Vow ye, and pay to the Lord our God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What ought we all in common to vow?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To believe in Him, to hope from Him for life eternal, to live godly according to a measure common to all.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For there is a certain measure common to all men.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To commit no theft is not a thing enjoined merely upon one devoted to continence, and not enjoined upon the married woman: to commit no adultery is enjoined upon all men: not to love wine-bibbing, whereby the soul is swallowed up, and doth corrupt in herself the Temple of God, is enjoined to all alike: not to be proud, is enjoined to all men alike: not to slay man, not to hate a brother, not to lay a plot to destroy any one, is enjoined to all in common.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The whole of this we all ought to vow.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There are also vows proper for individuals: one voweth to God conjugal chastity, that he will know no other woman besides his wife: so also the woman, that she will know no other man besides her husband.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Other men also vow, even though they have used such a marriage, that beyond this they will have no such thing, that they will neither desire nor admit the like: and these men have vowed a greater vow than the former.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Others vow even virginity from the beginning of life, that they will even know no such thing as those who having experienced have relinquished: and these men have vowed the greatest vow.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Others vow that their house shall be a place of entertainment for all the Saints that may come: a great vow they vow.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Another voweth to relinquish all his goods to be distributed to the poor, and go into a community, into a society of the Saints: a great vow he doth vow.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Vow ye, and pay to the Lord our God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let each one vow what he shall have willed to vow; let him give heed to this, that he pay what he hath vowed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If any man doth look back with regard to what he hath vowed to God, it is an evil.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Some woman or other devoted to continence hath willed to marry: what hath she willed?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The same as any virgin.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What hath she willed?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The same as her own mother.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hath she willed any evil thing?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Evil certainly.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because already she had vowed to the Lord her God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what hath the apostle Paul said concerning such?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Though he saith that young widows may marry if they will: nevertheless he saith in a certain passage, “but more blessed she will be, if so she shall have remained, after my judgment.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He showeth that she is more blessed, if so she shall have remained; but nevertheless that she is not to be condemned, if she shall have willed to marry.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But what saith he concerning certain who have vowed and have not paid?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Having,” he saith, “judgment, because the first faith they have made void.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, “the first faith they have made void”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They have vowed, and have not paid.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let no brother therefore, when placed in a monastery, say, I shall depart from the monastery: for neither are they only that are in a monastery to attain unto the kingdom of Heaven, nor do those that are not there not belong unto God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We answer him, but they have not vowed; thou hast vowed, thou hast looked back.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When the Lord was threatening them with the day of judgment, He saith what?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Remember Lot’s wife.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To all men He spake.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what did Lot’s wife?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: She was delivered from Sodom, and being in the way she looked back.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In the place where she looked back, there she remained.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For she became a statue of salt, in order that by considering her men might be seasoned, might have sense, might not be infatuated, might not look back, lest by giving a bad example they should themselves remain and season others.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For even now we are saying this to certain of our brethren, whom perchance we may have seen as it were weak in the good they have purposed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And wilt thou be such an one as he was?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We put before them certain who have looked back.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They are savourless in themselves, but they season others, inasmuch as they are mentioned, in order that fearing their example they may not look back.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Vow ye, and pay.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For that wife of Lot to all doth belong.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A married woman hath had the will to commit adultery; from her place whither she had arrived she looked back.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A widow who had vowed so to remain hath willed to marry, she hath willed the thing which was lawful to her who hath married, but to herself was not lawful, because from her place she hath looked back.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There is a virgin devoted to continence, already dedicated to God; let her have also the other gifts which truly do adorn virginity itself, and without which that virginity is unclean.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what if she be uncorrupt in body and corrupt in mind?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is it that he hath said?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What if no one hath touched the body, but if perchance she be drunken, be proud, be contentious, be talkative?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: All these things God doth condemn.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If before she had vowed, she had married, she would not have been condemned: she hath chosen something better, hath overcome that which was lawful for her; she is proud, and doth commit so many things unlawful.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This I say, it is lawful for her to marry before that she voweth, to be proud is never lawful.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: O thou virgin of God, thou hast willed not to marry, which is lawful: thou dost exalt thyself, which is not lawful.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Better is a virgin humble, than a married woman humble: but better is a married woman humble, than a virgin proud.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But she that looked back upon marriage is condemned, not because she hath willed to marry; but because she had already gone before, and is become the wife of Lot by looking back.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Be ye not slow, that are able, whom God doth inspire to seize upon higher callings: for we do not say these things in order that ye may not vow, but in order that ye may vow and may pay.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now because we have treated of these matters, thou perchance wast willing to vow, and now art not willing to vow.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But observe what the Psalm hath said to thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It hath not said, “Vow not;” but, “Vow and pay.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because thou hast heard, “pay,” wilt thou not vow?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore wast thou willing to vow, and not to pay?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Nay, do both.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: One thing is done by thy profession, another thing will be perfected by the aid of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Look to Him who doth guide thee, and thou wilt not look back to the place whence He is leading thee forth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He that guideth thee is walking before thee; the place from whence He is guiding thee is behind thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Love Him guiding, and He doth not condemn thee looking back.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “All they that are in the circuit of Him shall offer gifts.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who are in the circuit of Him?…Whatever is common to all is in the midst.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why is it said to be in the midst?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because it is at the same distance from all, and at the same proximity to all.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That which is not in the middle, is as it were private.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That which is public is set in the middle, in order that all they that come may use the same, may be enlightened.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let no one say, it is mine: lest he should be wanting to make his own share of that which is in the midst for all.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What then is, “All they that are in the circuit of Him shall offer gifts”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: All they that understand truth to be common to all, and who do not make it as it were their own by being proud concerning it, they shall offer gifts; because they have humility: but they that make as it were their own that which is common to all, as though it were set in the middle, are endeavouring to lead men astray to a party, these shall not offer gifts.…“To Him terrible.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let therefore all men fear that are in the circuit of Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For therefore they shall fear, and with trembling they shall praise; because they are in the circuit of Him, to the end that all men may attain unto Him, and He may openly meet all, and openly enlighten all.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is, to stand in awe with others.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When thou hast made him as it were thine own, and no longer common, thou art exalted unto pride; though it is written, “Serve ye the Lord in fear, and exult unto Him with trembling.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore they shall offer gifts, who are in the circuit of Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For they are humble who know truth to be common to all.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To whom shall they offer gifts?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “To Him terrible, and to Him that taketh away the spirit of princes” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the spirits of princes are proud spirits.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They then are not His Spirits; for if they know anything, their own they will it to be, not public; but, that which setteth Himself forth as equal toward all men, that setteth Himself in the midst, in order that all men may take as much as they can, whatever they can; not of what is any man’s, but of what is God’s, and therefore of their own because they have become His.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore they must needs be humble: they have lost their own spirit, and they have the Spirit of God.…For if thou shalt have confessed thyself dust, God out of dust doth make man.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: All they that are in the circuit of Him do offer gifts.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: All humble men do confess to Him, and do adore Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “To Him terrible they offer gifts.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whence to Him terrible exult ye with trembling: “and to Him that taketh away the spirit of princes:” that is, that taketh away the haughtiness of proud men.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “To Him terrible among the kings of the earth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Terrible are the kings of the earth, but He is above all, that doth terrify the kings of the earth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Be thou a king of the earth, and God will be to thee terrible.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How, wilt thou say, shall I be a king of the earth?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Rule the earth, and thou wilt be a king of the earth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Do not therefore with desire of empire set before thine eyes exceeding wide provinces, where thou mayest spread abroad thy kingdoms; rule thou the earth which thou bearest.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hear the Apostle ruling the earth: “I do not so fight as if beating air, but I chasten my body, and bring it into captivity, lest perchance preaching to other men, I myself become a reprobate.”…
